# @andes/snackbar

A snackbar is a fast feedback notification about a change or process.

There are two types of snackbars in this app:

a) Only text, without an action button.
b) Text with an action button.

For a11y reasons, we strongly suggest you don't use delay prop in a Snackbar that requires interaction, because of the users that need more time to reach the button or read the message. There are exceptions for this rule so you can use delay prop anyway.

The UI specs are defined in _[Andes UI](http://andes.adminml.com) > [Snackbar](https://andes.mercadolibre.com/924dc6ec5/p/791b82-snackbar/b/26f535)_.

## How to use

### Installation

> Follow the [general installation guide](../../docs/installation.md).

```bash
$ npm install --save @andes/snackbar
```

### Styles

> Follow the [general styles usage guide](../../docs/styles-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your bundle:

```scss
@import '@andes/snackbar/index';
```

Or you can import only the styles that you need in your project like this:

```scss
// Base
@import '@andes/snackbar/base';

// Positive
@import '@andes/snackbar/lib/styles/positive';

// Negative
@import '@andes/snackbar/lib/styles/negative';

// Desktop
@import  '@andes/snackbar/lib/styles/desktop';

// Mobile
@import '@andes/snackbar/lib/styles/mobile';

```
>  Always import `@andes/snackbar/base` and then only the styles that you need.

### Scripts

> Follow the [general scripts usage guide](../../docs/scripts-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your page:

```jsx
import { Snackbar } from '@andes/snackbar';
```

## Examples

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for Snackbar](https://andes.adminml.com/web/?path=/story/snackbar--andes-snackbar).

```jsx
<Snackbar
  message="Mensaje de Snackbar"
  color="positive"
  action={{ text: "Salida" }}
  show
  delay={3000}
/>
```

## Props

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `action` | Configuration element for the Snackbar action | `Object<ActionConfig>` | `undefined` |  |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `''` |  |
| `color` | <sup>1</sup>Color of the Snackbar, to show different states | `string: 'green', 'red', 'positive', 'negative'` | `undefined` |  |
| `delay` | Time in ms that the snackbar will be displayed, after those ms, the snackbar will be automatically hidden | number | `undefined` |  |
| `message` | Inner Snackbar message | `string` |  | ✅ |
| `onClose` | Callback executed once the snackbar is closed | `func()` | `'undefined'` |  |
| `onOpen` | Callback executed once the snackbar is open | `func()` | `'undefined'` |  |
| `show` | Determines whether the snackbar is visible or not | `boolean` | `'undefined'` |  |

#### ActionConfig

```jsx
  {
    text: string,
    position: oneOf(['right', 'bottom']),
    onClick: func,
  }
```

> 1 - `red` and `green` options will be removed from the 'color' options in a future major version. Please use `negative` or `positive` instead.

> Message wrap is only available for mobile

## Theming

This component supports theming. It allows you to pass a `customTheme` object to it adjusting colors, typography, spacing and styles to align with a brand's visual identity, user preferences or project requirements.

<br />

The `customTheme` object can make use of values ​​referenced in the component or CSS properties for quick customization or make use of style overrides according to `data-attributes` specific to the component for more complex cases.

<br />

### Using Referenced Component Values

Snackbar allows modifying the following attributes according to its `hierarchy`:

| Attribute                | Description                      |
| ------------------------ | -------------------------------- |
| `fill`                   | Snackbar fill color.             |
| `text`                   | Snackbar text color.             |
| `action`                 | Snackbar action color.           |

Taking this into account, the `customTheme` object has the following structure:

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    snackbar: {
      color: {
        fill: {
          [feedbackValue]: fillColorValue,
        },
        text: {
          [feedbackValue]: textColorValue,
        },
      },
      action: {
        color: {
          text: {
            [feedbackValue]: actionColorValue,
          }
        },
      },
    },
  },
};
```

The possible `feedbackValue` values ​​are:

```jsx

status: base | default | positive | negative;

```

> For a more detailed view of the structure of this object you can refer to the file `@andes/themes/meli-themes/ml.json`

#### Example

<img src="resources/snackbar/docs/Snackbar-Example-Reference-Theming.png" alt="Reference Theming Snackbar Example" width="680" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Snackbar } from '@andes/snackbar';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    snackbar: {
      color: {
        fill: {
          base: '#103969',
        },
        text: {
          base: '#f2f2f2',
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <Snackbar show message="Message" action={{ text: 'Action' }} />
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/snackbar/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Using CSS Properties

The component allows passing CSS properties following this structure:

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    snackbar: {
      [cssProperty]: propertyValue,
    },
  },
};
```

> Please note that styles applied in this way will impact all Andes snackbars regardless of their variant. Additionally, they have higher precedence than styles applied using referenced component values.

#### Example

<img src="resources/snackbar/docs/Snackbar-Example-CSS-Properties-Theming.png" alt="CSS Properties Theming Snackbar Example" width="680" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Snackbar } from '@andes/snackbar';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    snackbar: {
      background: '#477CFC',
      borderRadius: '3px',
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <Snackbar show message="Message" action={{ text: 'Action' }} />
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/snackbar/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Using `styleOverrides`

This component has a series of `data-attributes` that serve as a hook to style complex cases:

| data-attribute                       | Description                                                  |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `data-andes-snackbar`                | Snackbar tag.                                                |
| `data-andes-snackbar-color`          | Snackbar color. Can be: `'positive'` or `'negative'`.        |
| `data-andes-snackbar-message`        | Snackbar message tag.                                        |
| `data-andes-snackbar-content`        | Snackbar content tag.                                        |
| `data-andes-snackbar-action`         | Snackbar action tag.                                         |

Taking advantage of the them, you can use an entry called `styleOverrides` where you can add styles according to any `data-attribute`.

### Example

<img src="resources/snackbar/docs/Snackbar-Example-Style-Overrides-Theming.png" alt="Style Overrides Theming Snackbar Example" width="680" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Snackbar } from '@andes/snackbar';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    snackbar: {
      styleOverrides: {
        '&[data-andes-snackbar-color="negative"]': {
          background: '#F2E5E2',
          border: '2px solid #F1ABA9',
        },
        '[data-andes-snackbar-message]': {
          color: '#EB4337',
        },
        '[data-andes-snackbar-action]': {
          color: '#EB4337',
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <Snackbar color="negative" show message="Message" action={{ text: 'Action' }} />
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/snackbar/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.
